subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Time Dilation Near Black Holes
description: A 60-second lesson on Time Dilation Near Black Holes.

hook.text: Near a black hole, time slows down. A clock near the event horizon ticks slower than one far away.

buildup.text: Einstein's general relativity predicts this: stronger gravity means slower time. It's called gravitational time dilation.

discovery.text: GPS satellites must correct for time dilation caused by Earth's gravity. Near a black hole, the effect is extreme.

twist.text: Spending an hour near a black hole could mean years pass for everyone else—exactly as shown in the film Interstellar.

climax.text: At the event horizon, time stops from an outside observer's view. The falling person sees everything continue normally.

punchline.text: Time bends near black holes. One person's hour is another's century.

quiz.question: What happens to time near a black hole's event horizon?
options: Time slows dramatically compared to a distant observer
Time speeds up due to the intense energy
Time reverses direction inside the event horizon
